channel.title: Cards on Zhuofan Li
channel.description: Recent content in Cards on Zhuofan Li

item.title: Research
item.description: I am most interested in how organizations methodize and capitalize knowledge about human behavior. My dissertation examines how corporate participation in open science recasts knowledge infrastructure in computer vision, and other subfields of computer science that mobilize a transnational network of scientific labor force and massive human behavioral and biometric data. To answer this question, my research intersects network analysis, sociology and history of science, organizational theory, economic sociology, cultural sociology, and computational social science.

item.title: Recent
item.description: “Introduction to Machine Learning for Qualitative Research.”, with Corey Abramson and Daniel Dohan, Workshop at ASA 2022. [Slides] [Code] “The Temporal Structure of Capital Activation: Lessons from the Unsettled Lives of Terminal Cancer.” with Corey Abramson and Daniel Dohan, Paper to be presented at “Action in Unsettled Times”, Sociology of Culture Section Session, ASA 2022. [Slides] [Code] Awarded SBS Summer Dissertation Fellowship by the College of Social Behavioral Sciences at The University of Arizona.
